We stopped Truffle Line because the operator accidentally pressed an E-stop. We are currently taking a temper reading before restarting the line.

When an E-stop is activated, all equipment must be restarted within one minute. The operator responded as quickly as possible; however, he was at the beginning of the line and had to get to the other side of the enrober to complete the restart. Because the restart could not be completed within the one-minute window, the program had to be entered again in the enrober. Once the program is entered again, three acceptable temper readings are required before restarting the line. The first and second readings were good, and we are currently taking the third one.
The third temper  was good, and we restarted the line.
Third temper good

Please be attentive to the Magnet of the Temper unit, the vibration makes it move and the chocolate falls out of it, the bolt isn't holding very well and may be damaged. Preston Elmer Please can you give a solution to this?
The magnet should not be sitting on the screen. It was designed to sit at the top of the slide.

On closer inspection and interviews with the operator, we have some other issues as well. The screen over the buffer tank is missing a stopper that holds the magnet in place. They have a backup screen that DOES have the stopper, but the spot where the bolt holds it on is broken so it's set up the best way it can be, but we definitely need to fix the issues
Five bins of chocolate remained.
Why don't we put the magnet where it is designed to go? Laying in the screen it is effectively a "one" bar magnet instead of 5. Not very useful. Jenny Medina Preston Tregaskis

We had a chocolate spill on North side sweco, we’re investigating how if temper unit is still in Nightmode

The PTL, backoff and licking, and temper unit flow into this. Is it possible the PTL was activated and began pumping? Also, I know the ML1 temper unit has a night mode shutdown timer, the circulating pump runs for 60min after night mode is selected. I have never noticed whether or not the sollich units do something similar, but that would go back through the sweco on this machine if the turbotempers do have a similar feature

Everyone this was noticed yesterday. It's the flanged bearing for adjusting the angle of the inclusion incline conveyor. So that means BOTH ends of the crank screw are now broken. Chad Stone was present when the first one broke. It's important that we continue to communicate to the operators the general practice, knowledge and understanding on the equipment of:


 


If it feels harder to move, if there is resistance, if it's not moving at all... you have to take a moment to see why. Is it jammed on something? Have you reached the maximum travel of the mechanism? Is there a physical latch or lock in place preventing movement? Is it already broken or damaged in some way? Should I call maintenance now before I damage it or create more damage?


 


If we keep communicating this, I know we will improve. Thank you.

Itzel Chavez

Did we ever get labeling on the area where we move the lever to raise / lower?

Hablé con el supervisor y, según lo que hemos observado, el problema parece estar relacionado con el volumen de producto que se acumula sobre la banda transportadora. Cuando dos pouches pasan al mismo tiempo por el equipo de rayos X (XR), el sistema los rechaza automáticamente.

Además, al incrementar la velocidad de las bandas, esta situación se vuelve más frecuente. Considero que deberíamos evaluar la posibilidad de instalar un equipo de rayos X adicional para manejar el flujo de producción de manera más eficiente y reducir los rechazos ocasionados por esta condición.
Running at 1.1 we are doing 1,300ppm our goal for the day is 900ppm. We are running 400ppm over. Curt Willis

We’re pausing the line for 20 minutes to pass accumulation.

We are currently running at 1.1 at 50 cuts x26 rows of caramel = 1300 pieces a minutes. 

However, we have an almost a full pallet of wip that we need to pass.

We’re are reducing the line speed to 1.0. This will still have us above attainment.

After bringing the run rate to 1.0, our piece count is 1,196 = 46x26 

Attainment is 54,000/hr or 
900 pieces/min.

We’ll see if running at this rate helps us not put into wip, and also pause less once we’ve passed accumulation.
We are going to take a pause  of 30 minutes; we have 27 containers of accumulated product.

We’re are starting the line back up and will pass accumulation into wip and continue running without pause. 

Then label the pallets as wip, and run later.

We just finished a call to discuss lab results from the XR80 run.  Here are the topics discussed and actions

All ACP tests prior to the XR80 run passed

The one shot caramel that was ran after the XR80 run passed all APC testing 

Finished product from the XR80 run failed ACP testing 

We believe that the support technicians on sight might have not worn gloves when making adjustments which resulted in contamination.  Or the test results were incorrect to start with.

The product is being retested now and results should be back tomorrow afternoon or night.  

We are treating this as though it will fail APC again so we are taking the following actions


1.  Production will reclean the line from waste high up.  This is being started now and we are targeting tomorrow completion.

2.  QA will take ACP tests after cleaning and send them out tomorrow to target results being back Tuesday.

If retest fails, than

3.  Production will run 1 batch of XR80 though the twist wrap and samples will be sent out for testing to get additional information on wrapped product and not just the line itself.  Line could pass, product could fail again.

If retest passes we will regroup on Monday and decide if we need to run this batch.


Tuesdays run will stay as scheduled.

Aubrey Howe Ira Robison Miriah Manzanares Candon Watt. We are all in agreement on this plan correct

Chad Stone FYI
